title: circaea alpina subsp. alpina
intro: Circaea alpina subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Enchanter's Nightshade or Alpine Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the family Lythraceae, characterized by its small white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Circaea alpina subsp. alpina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the dried leaves in tinctures and infusions.
